The fix has been correctly applied to `src/app.ts`. Verifying the details:

1. ✅ `searchInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Search files');` is added immediately after the placeholder is set (line 77, right after `searchInput.placeholder = 'Search...';`)
2. ✅ The placeholder is preserved (`searchInput.placeholder = 'Search...';` remains intact — needed for the empty-state hint and the `:placeholder-shown` clear-button toggle)
3. ✅ Uses the exact value `'Search files'` specified in the instructions
4. ✅ Matches the existing pattern — the sibling upload input uses `uploadInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Upload files')`, and other icon buttons (close, clear) follow the same `setAttribute('aria-label', ...)` convention
5. ✅ Includes a clear, accurate comment explaining the rationale (placeholder is unreliable, WCAG 1.3.1/4.1.2, matching the upload input)
6. ✅ No existing test asserts the *absence* of an aria-label on the search input. The relevant tests in `src/app/start-app.test.ts` (lines 100-107) only check `type`, `placeholder`, and DOM structure — none would break, and none required updating.

The fix is minimal, targeted, and follows the instructions exactly. No scope expansion, no dead code, consistent with project conventions.

```json
{"approved": true, "feedback": "The fix is correctly and completely applied. In src/app.ts, `searchInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Search files');` was added immediately after `searchInput.placeholder = 'Search...';` (around line 77), exactly per the fix instructions. The placeholder is preserved as required (it doubles as the empty-state hint and drives the `:placeholder-shown` clear-button toggle). The aria-label value 'Search files' matches the instruction verbatim. The change is consistent with the existing project pattern: the sibling upload input uses `uploadInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Upload files')`, and the close/clear icon buttons use the same `setAttribute('aria-label', ...)` mechanism. A concise, accurate comment explains the WCAG 1.3.1/4.1.2 rationale and notes it matches the upload input. No test asserts the absence of an aria-label on the search input (start-app.test.ts lines 100-107 only assert type, placeholder, and structure), so no test updates were needed. No dead code, no scope expansion, no logic errors.", "issues": []}
```
